title: The Person and Work of Christ, Part 5
speaker: Elder Nick Anderson
source_files: 5 - 12 - 24 - Person And Work Of Christ - Part 5 - Elder Nick Anderson.txt

outline.title: Introduction: Westminster Larger Catechism Q. 44
subpoints: Q. 44: How does Christ execute the office of a priest?

Answer: Christ executes the office of a priest in His once offering Himself a sacrifice without spot to God, to be a reconciliation for the sins of His people, and in making continual intercession for them

Four requirements of the priestly office: (1) taken from among men; (2) chosen by God; (3) holy, pure, and consecrated to the Lord; (4) the right to draw near to God and offer sacrifice and intercession

outline.title: Was Christ Actually a Priest?
subpoints: Psalm 110:4: 'The Lord has sworn and will not relent: You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek'
Hebrews 5:6 and 6:19-20 quote this, calling Jesus 'high priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek'
Isaiah 53:10-12: He makes His soul an offering for sin, bears their iniquities, makes intercession for the transgressors — all priestly functions
All Levitical temple services were typological of what Christ would do as our great high priest

outline.title: Christ Fulfills the Requirements of the Priestly Office
subpoints: He was taken from among men: Hebrews 2:17 — 'In all things He had to be made like His brethren, that He might be a merciful and faithful high priest... to make propitiation for the sins of the people'
He was chosen by God: Hebrews 5:5-6 — 'Christ did not glorify Himself to become high priest, but it was He who said to Him, You are My Son... You are a priest forever'
He was perfectly holy: Luke 1:35 ('the holy one who is to be born'); Hebrews 7:26 ('holy, harmless, undefiled, separate from sinners, higher than the heavens')

He had the greatest access to the Father: John 16:28; John 11:42; Hebrews 1:3; Hebrews 9:24 — 'Christ has not entered the holy places made with hands... but into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God for us'
He sympathizes with our weaknesses: Hebrews 4:15 — 'in all points tempted as we are, yet without sin'

outline.title: Christ Performed the Duties of the Priestly Office
subpoints: He mediates generally: John 14:6; 1 Timothy 2:5 — 'There is one God and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus'

He mediates by offering propitiation: Hebrews 9:26 — 'He has appeared to put away sin by the sacrifice of Himself'; Hebrews 10:12 — 'this Man, after He had offered one sacrifice for sins forever, sat down at the right hand of God'
The Catholic Mass, which claims to re-sacrifice Christ repeatedly, is an abomination — Christ's sacrifice was one time for all time
He mediates by intercession: Romans 8:34; Hebrews 7:25 — 'He is also able to save to the uttermost those who come to God through Him, since He always lives to make intercession for them'
His priestly work was performed both on earth (obedience, propitiation, prayer) and in heaven (presenting His sacrifice, continual intercession)

outline.title: How Christ's Priesthood Is Greater Than the Aaronic Priesthood
subpoints: Dignity of person: the Aaronic priests were sinful men; Christ is the eternal Son of God, holy, pure, undefiled
Value of sacrifice: the animal sacrifices had to be repeated endlessly and could never cleanse (Hebrews 10:1-4); Christ's one sacrifice was perfect and perfectly efficacious (Hebrews 10:14)
Manner of consecration: the Aaronic priests were consecrated without an oath; Christ's was confirmed by God's oath (Hebrews 7:20-22; Psalm 110:4)
Duration: the Aaronic priests were many, dying generation after generation; Christ's priesthood is one and continues forever (Hebrews 7:24)
Tabernacle: Christ ministers in the true heavenly tabernacle, not a copy made with hands (Hebrews 9:24)
Intercession from a throne: Christ intercedes from the right hand of God (Romans 8:34)
Uniqueness: Only Christ was simultaneously prophet, priest, and king in divine perfection; others held two offices at most

outline.title: The Christian Ministry Is Not a Priesthood
subpoints: Human priests were only ever types; the Aaronic priesthood was fulfilled and abolished in Christ
Christ exhaustively discharges all the duties of the priestly office — any so-called human priest is an antichrist, someone trying to take the place of Christ
No priestly function is given to any officer in the New Testament: 1 Corinthians 12:28; Ephesians 4:11-12; the qualifications of elders and deacons in 1 Timothy 3 include no priestly role
We come to the Father through Christ alone: John 14:6; Matthew 11:28; Revelation 22:17

outline.title: In What Sense Are All Believers Priests?
subpoints: Because of our union with Christ, every believer participates in the priesthood of their Head
This does not mean we offer expiatory sacrifice; it means we have immediate access to God and can offer spiritual sacrifices: praise, prayer, intercession (Hebrews 13:15; 1 Timothy 2:1-2)
1 Peter 2:5: 'You also, as living stones, are being built up a spiritual house, a holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God through Jesus Christ'
1 Peter 2:9: 'But you are a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, a holy nation' — to proclaim the praises of Him who called you out of darkness
By the same union with Christ, we are also prophets and kings in fellowship with Christ (Revelation 1:6; 5:10)

summary: This is Part 5 of the Person and Work of Christ Sunday school series, covering Westminster Larger Catechism Question 44. Elder Nick Anderson addresses Christ's execution of the priestly office by first establishing what a priest is: taken from among men, chosen by God, holy and consecrated, and with the right to draw near to God and offer sacrifice and intercession. He demonstrates from Psalm 110:4, Isaiah 53, and the book of Hebrews that Christ was indeed called a priest — specifically the high priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek — and that all the functions of the priestly office are ascribed to Him. He shows that Christ fulfills every qualification: fully human, divinely appointed, perfectly holy, and possessing the greatest possible access to the Father. He then distinguishes Christ's one-for-all sacrifice from the Aaronic system of repeated animal offerings, directly refuting the Catholic Mass. He explains six ways Christ's priesthood exceeds the Aaronic: the dignity of His person, the infinite value of His sacrifice, the oath-confirmed nature of His consecration, the perpetuity of His single-person priesthood, His ministry in the true heavenly tabernacle, and His intercession from the very throne of God. He then argues that no human Christian minister holds a priestly office — any such claim is a form of antichrist — while affirming that all believers, by union with Christ, constitute a royal priesthood in a spiritual sense, offering sacrifices of praise and intercession.
transcript:
## Introduction: What Does a Priest Do?

For our adult Sunday school, we have been talking about Christ, His person and work. Last week, Pastor Tito covered how Christ executes the office of a prophet, and this morning I will be covering Question 44 of the Larger Catechism, which is how Christ executes the office of a priest.

Question 44 asks: How does Christ execute the office of a priest? It answers: Christ executes the office of a priest in His once offering Himself a sacrifice without spot to God, to be a reconciliation for the sins of His people, and in making continual intercession for them.

Before we talk about Christ Himself, we need to answer the question: What did the priestly office actually consist of? What did the priest do according to the Scriptures? Hebrews 5:1 gives us a good starting point: "For every high priest taken from among men is appointed for men in things pertaining to God, that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins."

So first, he must be taken from among men — this is one reason why Christ had to be a real man. Second, he must be chosen by God to be His special servant. Hebrews 5:4: "And no man takes this honor to himself, but he who is called by God, just as Aaron was." Numbers 16, the rebellion of Korah, illustrates what happens when men appoint themselves: the earth swallowed them. Third, they must be holy, pure, and consecrated to the Lord. Leviticus 21:6-8: "They shall be holy to their God... therefore you shall consecrate him, for he offers the bread of your God. He shall be holy to you, for I the Lord, who sanctify you, am holy." Exodus 39:31: they made the plate of the holy crown of pure gold, engraved with "Holiness to the Lord." Fourth, they have the right to draw near to God and to bring sacrifice and make intercession. Exodus 19:22: "Also, let the priests who come near the Lord consecrate themselves, lest the Lord break out against them."

Leviticus 16 describes how Aaron would come into the holy place with the blood of a young bull and a ram, offer it as a sin offering, and burn incense representative of prayers. All these things described in Leviticus 16 were typical of what Christ would do as our great high priest.

So a priest is a mediator — taken from among men to stand before God for the purpose of making propitiation by sacrifice and for the purpose of offering prayers, which we call intercession.

## Was Christ Actually a Priest?

Now we have established what a priest does, we need to see whether Christ was actually a priest. An easy place to go is Psalm 110:4 compared with Hebrews 5:6 and 6:19-20. Psalm 110:4: "The Lord has sworn and will not relent: You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek." Hebrews 5:6 quotes this and says, "You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek." Hebrews 6:19-20: "This hope we have as an anchor of the soul, both sure and steadfast, which enters the presence behind the veil, where the forerunner has entered for us, even Jesus, having become high priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek."

Isaiah 53:10-12 also describes priestly functions ascribed to Christ: "It pleased the Lord to bruise Him; He has put Him to grief. When You make His soul an offering for sin, He shall see His seed... My righteous servant shall justify many, for He shall bear their iniquities... He bore the sin of many, and made intercession for the transgressors." We see Him offering Himself as an offering, bearing iniquities, and making intercession — all priestly functions.

## Christ Fulfills the Requirements of the Priestly Office

Christ meets every requirement of the priestly office. First, He was taken from among men to represent them before God. Hebrews 2:17: "Therefore, in all things He had to be made like His brethren, that He might be a merciful and faithful high priest in things pertaining to God, to make propitiation for the sins of the people." This is why denying the full humanity of Christ is so serious — if He was not truly a man, He could not fulfill the office of priest.

Hebrews 4:15: "For we do not have a high priest who cannot sympathize with our weaknesses, but was in all points tempted as we are, yet without sin." This gives believers tremendous comfort: Jesus really knows our weaknesses; He experienced life the same way we do.

Second, Christ was chosen by God. Hebrews 5:5-6: "So also Christ did not glorify Himself to become high priest, but it was He who said to Him, 'You are My Son, today I have begotten You.' As He also says in another place, 'You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek.'" Jesus did not take this upon Himself — the Father chose Him.

Third, Christ was perfectly holy. Luke 1:35: the angel says to Mary, "The holy one who is to be born will be called the Son of God." Hebrews 7:26: "For such a High Priest was fitting for us: who is holy, harmless, undefiled, separate from sinners, and has become higher than the heavens."

Fourth, He had the greatest access — the nearest access and greatest influence with the Father. John 10:30: "I and My Father are one." John 11:42: "I know that You always hear Me." Hebrews 9:24: "For Christ has not entered the holy places made with hands, which are copies of the true, but into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God for us." Unlike earthly priests who ministered in the temple, Christ is not merely a man — He is God Himself, with the greatest access to the Father.

There are no super Christians, no priests who have greater access to the Father than you or I. All of us, in Christ, have the exact same access to the Father because of our union with Him.

## Christ Performed the Duties of the Priestly Office

Christ mediates in a general sense. John 14:6: "I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through Me." 1 Timothy 2:5: "For there is one God and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus." There is no need for an earthly priest anymore, because Christ is our high priest.

He mediates by offering propitiation. Hebrews 9:26: "Now, once at the end of the ages, He has appeared to put away sin by the sacrifice of Himself." Hebrews 10:12: "But this Man, after He had offered one sacrifice for sins forever, sat down at the right hand of God."

Here is an important side note: this verse explicitly states that Christ's sacrifice was one time. The Catholic Mass, which claims to sacrifice Christ over and over again, is nothing more than a monstrous, abominable, idolatrous thing. Christ was offered one time for all time.

He also mediates by His intercession — and we will have a whole lesson on intercession in a few weeks. Romans 8:34: "Who is he who condemns? It is Christ who died, and furthermore is also risen, who is even at the right hand of God, who also makes intercession for us." Hebrews 7:25: "Therefore He is also able to save to the uttermost those who come to God through Him, since He always lives to make intercession for them." 1 John 2:1: "My little children, these things I write to you, so that you may not sin. And if anyone sins, we have an Advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous."

If we sin, we have an Advocate with the Father. It is not as though God is looking down on us angry because we sinned yet again. Jesus wants us to pray for strength against that sin for the ten-thousandth time, so that we can actually repent. He is our Advocate with the Father.

## How Christ's Priesthood Is Greater Than the Aaronic

First, the dignity of the person. The Aaronic priests were mere men; Christ is the eternal Son of God, holy, pure, undefiled. They had to make atonement for themselves before making atonement for others; He had no such need.

Second, the value of Christ's sacrifice is infinite. Hebrews 10:1-4: "For the law, having a shadow of the good things to come... can never with these same sacrifices, which they offer continually year by year, make those who approach perfect. For then would they not have ceased to be offered?... For it is not possible that the blood of bulls and goats could take away sins." On the other hand, Christ's sacrifice was perfect and perfectly efficacious, once for all. Hebrews 10:14: "By one offering He has perfected forever those who are being sanctified."

Third, the manner of their consecration. The Aaronic priests were consecrated without an oath; Christ's was with an oath. Hebrews 7:20-22: "In as much as He was not made priest without an oath... but He with an oath by Him who said to Him, 'The Lord has sworn and will not relent: You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek.'" By so much more Jesus has become a surety of a better covenant.

Fourth, they were many — generation after generation of priests — but His is one and continues forever. Hebrews 7:24: "But He, because He continues forever, has an unchangeable priesthood."

Fifth, Christ's priesthood is connected with a greater and better tabernacle. Hebrews 9:24: He has entered into heaven itself.

Sixth, Christ's intercession is offered from a throne. Romans 8:34: He is at the right hand of God, making intercession for us.

Seventh, only Christ in divine perfection was at once prophet, priest, and king. Others held two offices at most — David was both prophet and king; Ezra was both prophet and priest. But none but Christ was all three.

## The Christian Ministry Is Not a Priesthood

First, human priests in the Old Testament were only ever types — they were not the real thing. The Aaronic priesthood was fulfilled in Christ and therefore abolished. Second, Christ exhaustively discharges all the duties of the priestly office in Himself, so that any so-called human priest is actually an antichrist — someone who is trying to take the place of Christ. When someone claims to be a priest with closer access to God, they are in a sense an antichrist. Colossians 2:10: "And you are complete in Him who is the head of all principality and power." Third, there is no priestly function given to any officer in the New Testament. 1 Corinthians 12:28, Ephesians 4:11-12, and the qualifications for elders and deacons in 1 Timothy 3 give no priestly role.

The New Testament always calls people to come directly to Christ: "Come to Me, all you who labor" (Matthew 11:28); "Behold, I stand at the door and knock" (Revelation 3:20); "Let him who thirsts come, whoever desires, let him take the water of life freely" (Revelation 22:17).

## In What Sense Are All Believers Priests?

Because of their union with Christ, every believer has a part in the priesthood of their Head — but not in offering expiatory sacrifice. We are a royal priesthood in the sense that we have immediate access to God through Christ and can offer spiritual sacrifices. Hebrews 13:15: "Therefore by Him let us continually offer the sacrifice of praise to God, that is, the fruit of our lips, giving thanks to His name." 1 Timothy 2:1: "I exhort first of all that supplications, prayers, intercessions, and giving of thanks be made for all men."

1 Peter 2:5: "You also, as living stones, are being built up a spiritual house, a holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God through Jesus Christ." 1 Peter 2:9: "But you are a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, His own special people, that you may proclaim the praises of Him who called you out of darkness into His marvelous light."

By the same union with Christ, we are also prophets and kings. 1 John 2:20: "But you have an anointing from the Holy One, and you know all things." Revelation 1:6: "He has made us kings and priests to His God and Father." Revelation 5:10: "And have made us kings and priests to our God, and we shall reign on the earth."

Our gracious God, we thank you for the Lord Jesus Christ. We thank you for everything that He has done for us. We thank you that He was willing to take on human flesh, willing to suffer and die for us, willing to fulfill all the offices of prophet, priest, and king. We thank you that He stands before You making intercession for us, so that no matter what we struggle with, we can always come to the Father because of what our great Lord Jesus has done for us. We thank you for your goodness and your mercy, and we pray that you bless the rest of our day today. We ask this in Christ's name. Amen.
